evals.prompt: I need to add a new feature section for 'Lagerverwaltung' (Inventory Management) to the app. What translation keys do I need to add and where? Show me the exact entries for both de.js and en.js.
evals.expected_output: Complete set of translation keys for inventory section in both DE and EN, following naming conventions.

assertions.name: de-js-referenced
assertions.description: References js/i18n/de.js as target file

assertions.name: en-js-referenced
assertions.description: References js/i18n/en.js as target file

assertions.name: dot-notation-keys
assertions.description: Uses dot notation like inventory.* or lager.*

assertions.name: german-translations
assertions.description: German text includes Lagerverwaltung or Lager

assertions.name: english-translations
assertions.description: English text includes Inventory or Stock

assertions.name: both-languages-shown
assertions.description: Shows entries for BOTH de and en files

assertions.name: data-i18n-usage
assertions.description: Mentions using data-i18n attribute in HTML

assertions.name: window-t-usage
assertions.description: Mentions window.t() for JS usage

assertions.name: apply-translations
assertions.description: Mentions calling applyTranslations() after dynamic rendering

assertions.name: no-html-in-values
assertions.description: Translation values are plain text, no HTML tags

evals.prompt: I think we have some translation keys in de.js that are missing from en.js. How do I find and fix these gaps? Also check if there are any parameter mismatches between the two files.
evals.expected_output: Method to find missing keys between DE/EN, parameter mismatch detection, and fix approach.

assertions.name: key-comparison-method
assertions.description: Shows how to compare keys between de.js and en.js (Object.keys, diff, or similar)

assertions.name: flatten-approach
assertions.description: Mentions flattening nested objects for comparison

assertions.name: parameter-check
assertions.description: Mentions checking {{param}} consistency between languages

assertions.name: plural-form-check
assertions.description: Mentions checking plural forms (one/other) consistency

assertions.name: fallback-behavior
assertions.description: Explains what happens when key is missing (shows raw key string)

assertions.name: both-files-referenced
assertions.description: References both de.js and en.js file paths

assertions.name: fix-approach
assertions.description: Provides approach to fix gaps (add missing keys)

assertions.name: no-auto-translate
assertions.description: Does NOT suggest using Google Translate or auto-translation for production

evals.prompt: How does the landing page translation system work differently from the app's i18n? I want to add a new section to the landing page in all supported languages.
evals.expected_output: Explains the dual translation system: data-lang (landing) vs data-i18n (app), with correct count of landing languages.

assertions.name: data-lang-explained
assertions.description: Explains data-lang attribute system for landing page

assertions.name: data-i18n-explained
assertions.description: Explains data-i18n attribute system for app

assertions.name: two-systems
assertions.description: Clearly states these are two separate/different systems

assertions.name: language-count
assertions.description: Mentions 31 languages for landing page

assertions.name: inline-blocks
assertions.description: Mentions inline language blocks or spans per language

assertions.name: app-only-de-en
assertions.description: States app translations are only DE and EN

assertions.name: landing-html-file
assertions.description: References landing.html as the file

assertions.name: shows-html-example
assertions.description: Shows HTML example with data-lang spans
